experiments.before: /barcode-scanner → /features (generic, irrelevant target)
experiments.after: /barcode-scanner → /tools/qr-code-decoder (specific, relevant tool)
experiments.hypothesis: Redirecting to a relevant tool page instead of generic /features should recover click-through from users searching for barcode/QR scanning tools
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 10 clicks, 508 impr, 2.0% CTR, pos 9.2. vs baseline: CTR +23% (1.62→2.0), position improved 15.7→9.2. Impressions down (seasonal or Google re-evaluating). Redirect working as intended.

experiments.before: The Open Source Asset Tracking Platform | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.after: Open Source Asset Management Software — Free for Teams | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.hypothesis: 8,248 monthly impressions for 'asset management software' with 0.0% CTR. Current title says 'Asset Tracking' not 'Asset Management' and doesn't mention 'free'. Matching the search intent + adding 'free' should lift CTR.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 465 clicks, 35,073 impr, 1.3% CTR, pos 13.0. vs baseline: CTR +65% (0.79→1.3), position improved 16→13. Strong signal — 'Asset Management Software' + 'Free' in title is working. Homepage is the highest-leverage page.

experiments.before: Track equipment across people, locations, and workflows with QR-first simplicity, custody logging, and modern workflows.
experiments.after: Track who has what, where it is, and when it's due back. QR scanning, custody logs, and booking calendars — free for individuals, open source.
experiments.hypothesis: 5,372 impressions at 0.2% CTR and pos 30.9. More conversational tone + mentioning 'free' and 'open source' in the meta description should make the listing more clickable and differentiate from paid competitors.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 14 clicks, 2,012 impr, 0.7% CTR, pos 16.1. vs baseline: CTR +180% (0.25→0.7), position improved dramatically 30.3→16.1. Conversational description + 'free' + 'open source' is a winning formula. Biggest position jump of batch 1.

experiments.before: Equipment Check-In/Check-Out Software | Shelf
experiments.after: Equipment Check-In & Check-Out Software — QR Scan, Track, Return | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 6,877 impressions at 0.4% CTR and pos 19.0. Adding action verbs (scan, track, return) makes the listing more compelling and concrete in search results vs the generic current title.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 19 clicks, 2,839 impr, 0.7% CTR, pos 10.9. vs baseline: CTR +150% (0.28→0.7), position improved 19.2→10.9. Action verbs in title working. Page moved from page 2 to page 1.

experiments.before: No page exists — /macrs-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive MACRS depreciation calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 8,320 monthly impressions going to a generic /tools page. Building a dedicated calculator should capture this traffic and feed users into the Shelf funnel via CTAs.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 138 clicks, 9,452 impr, 1.5% CTR, pos 11.6. Top query: 'macrs depreciation calculator' (25 clicks, pos 6.6). PostHog: 26 views/14d. GSC still reports old URL — 301 working but not yet re-indexed.

experiments.before: No page exists — /equipment-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive equipment depreciation calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 7,494 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated page with straight-line depreciation calculator should capture this traffic.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 27 clicks, 7,950 impr, 0.3% CTR, pos 16.8. Weakest performer of the 3 calculators — deeper position and lower CTR. May benefit from title/description optimization. PostHog: 7 views/14d.

experiments.before: No page exists — /salvage-value-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive salvage value calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 8,163 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated calculator page should capture this high-volume traffic.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 73 clicks, 8,358 impr, 0.9% CTR, pos 9.3. Best CTR of the 3 calculators. Top query: 'salvage value calculator' (22 clicks, pos 2.2). PostHog: 8 views/14d.

experiments.before: Snipe-IT Alternative — Modern Open-Source Asset Management | Shelf
experiments.after: Snipe-IT vs Shelf — Which Open Source Asset Manager Fits Your Team? (2026)
experiments.hypothesis: 1,522 impressions at 0.9% CTR, pos 10.0. The 'vs' format with a year tag performs better for comparison queries — makes the listing feel like a current, objective comparison rather than biased marketing.
experiments.learnings: Title change was deployed in PR #33. Code already live — marking deployedAt retroactively.

experiments.before: Free Asset Label Designer (Scan-Safe) | Shelf
experiments.after: Free Asset Tag Maker & Label Designer | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 128 clicks/30d from /asset-tag-maker (18K impr, pos 24.8) + 'asset tag generator free' (7 clicks, 19.4% CTR). Current title says 'Label Designer' but searchers use 'asset tag' terminology. Matching search intent should lift CTR on tag-related queries.
experiments.learnings: Title change was deployed in PR #33. Code already live — marking deployedAt retroactively.

experiments.before: Title: 'Equipment Management Software — Free QR Tracking & Scheduling | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free equipment management software with QR scanning, custody tracking, booking calendars, and location management. Open source. Used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Free Equipment Management Software — Open Source | Shelf' / Desc: 'Manage shared equipment with QR scanning, booking calendars, and custody tracking. Free for individuals, no per-asset pricing. Open source — used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.hypothesis: SERP analysis: Page 1 for 'equipment management software' is all paid enterprise tools (ToolSense, Fleetio, Cheqroom, Samsara). None are free/open-source. Leading with 'Free' and 'no per-asset pricing' differentiates from every competitor on page 1. Batch 1 learnings: 'free' + 'open source' in descriptions drove +150-180% CTR lifts.

experiments.before: Free, open-source asset management with QR tracking, custody logs, and booking calendars. Sign up and start tracking in minutes. AGPL licensed. 3,000+ teams.
experiments.after: Free, open-source asset management — cloud-hosted or self-host. QR tracking, custody logs, and booking calendars. Start tracking in minutes, no setup required. AGPL licensed. 3,000+ teams.
experiments.hypothesis: SERP analysis: Snipe-IT dominates 'open source asset management' at #1 but is self-host only (requires PHP/MySQL setup). Our cloud+self-host option is unique. Replaced jargon 'No DevOps required' with clearer 'Start tracking in minutes, no setup required'. CTR already 3.3% — targeting position improvement via relevance signal.

experiments.before: Title: 'Camera Equipment Check-Out Software — QR Checkout for Film & Media | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free camera equipment checkout software for film programs and media teams. QR scanning, booking calendars, kit tracking. Open source. Used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Camera Equipment Checkout Software — QR Scan & Book | Shelf' / Desc: 'Camera gear checkout for film programs, media departments, and production houses. QR scan, book, track custody — no sign-out sheets. Open source, free to start. 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.hypothesis: 3,988 impr (30d), 0.6% CTR, pos 18.1. Removed misleading 'Free for Teams' (free tier is single-user). Kept action verbs ('scan, book, track') and pain point ('no sign-out sheets') from exp-004 success (+150% CTR). 'Free to start' is accurate — free tier exists for individuals.

experiments.before: Title: 'Asset Tracking Software — Free QR Scanning & Custody Tracking | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free asset tracking software with QR scanning, custody logs, booking calendars, and location tracking. Open source. Used by 3,000+ teams to track 450,000+ assets.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Free Asset Tracking Software — Open Source QR Scanning | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free asset tracking software with QR scanning, custody logs, and location tracking. Unlimited assets, open source. Used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.hypothesis: 5,339 impr, 0.4% CTR, pos 23.9. Two changes: (1) Lead with 'Free' — batch 1 proved this lifts CTR. (2) Remove 'booking calendars' from description — bookings is a Team plan feature ($67/mo), not free. Replaced with 'Unlimited assets' which IS true on free tier. Truthfulness fix + CTR optimization.

experiments.before: Title: 'IT Asset Management Software — Free Device Tracking for IT Teams | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free IT asset management software with QR scanning, custody tracking, and device lifecycle management. Track laptops, tablets, and peripherals across hybrid teams. Open source.'
experiments.after: Title: 'IT Asset Management Software — Free, Open Source | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free, open-source IT asset management. QR scanning, custody tracking, and device lifecycle management for laptops, tablets, and peripherals. Self-host or cloud. AGPL licensed.'
experiments.hypothesis: Removed 'for IT Teams' from title — free tier is single-user, not teams. Removed 'across hybrid teams' from description — same misleading implication. Added 'Self-host or cloud' to differentiate from Snipe-IT (#1 SERP) which is self-host only. SERP: Snipe-IT dominates, Shelf already has 2 results — need clearer differentiation.

experiments.before: Fixed Asset Tracking Software — Free QR-Based Lifecycle Tracking | Shelf
experiments.after: Free Fixed Asset Tracking Software — QR Labels & Lifecycle Management | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 1,082 impr, 1.4% CTR, pos 15.8. Leading with 'Free' (batch 1 learning). SERP dominated by accounting tools (Sage, Intuit, MIP) — Shelf differentiates as operational tracking with QR labels. Shelf already #3, pushing for #1-2.

experiments.before: Title: 'Tool Tracking Software — Free QR-Based Field Tracking | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free tool tracking software for field teams. QR scanning, custody chains, and location tracking across job sites, trucks, and crews. Open source. 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Free Tool Tracking Software — QR Scan, Assign, Track | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free tool tracking software with QR scanning, custody chains, and location tracking across job sites, trucks, and crews. Open source. Start with one user, scale to your whole team.'
experiments.hypothesis: SERP dominated by construction-specific tools (ToolHound, ShareMyToolbox, TOOLTRIBE). Shelf is broader. Changes: (1) Lead with 'Free' (batch 1). (2) Action verbs 'Scan, Assign, Track' (exp-004 +150%). (3) Fixed 'for field teams' → 'Start with one user, scale to your whole team' — honest about free tier being single-user while showing growth path.

experiments.before: Sortly Alternative — Equipment Tracking Beyond Inventory Lists | Shelf
experiments.after: Sortly Alternative (2026) — Equipment Tracking Beyond Inventory Lists | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 1,894 impr, 1.7% CTR, pos 6.4. Already #2 behind G2. Every competitor on SERP has year tags (G2 'in 2026', Capterra '2026', Software Advice '2026'). Adding (2026) signals freshness and matches the pattern users expect for comparison queries.

experiments.before: Title: 'Equipment Reservation Software — Free Booking & Scheduling | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free equipment reservation software with conflict-free booking calendars. Prevent double-bookings for cameras, tools, laptops, and AV gear. Open source. 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Equipment Reservation Software — Prevent Double-Bookings | Shelf' / Desc: 'Equipment reservation software with conflict-free booking calendars. Prevent double-bookings for cameras, tools, laptops, and AV gear. Open source. No per-asset fees.'
experiments.hypothesis: Already #1 for 'equipment reservation software'. TRUTHFULNESS FIX: Removed 'Free' from title and description — bookings/reservations is a Team plan feature ($67/mo), NOT available on free tier. Replaced with value prop 'Prevent Double-Bookings' and 'No per-asset fees' which are both true.

experiments.before: Title: 'Educational Resource Management Software | Shelf' / Desc: 'Track cameras, laptops, AV gear, maker tools, and lab equipment across departments with simple QR-based workflows.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Educational Equipment Tracking Software — QR-Based Campus Management | Shelf' / Desc: 'Track cameras, laptops, AV gear, and lab equipment across departments and campuses. QR scanning, custody logs, and booking calendars. Open source, free to start.'
experiments.hypothesis: Current title is generic with no keywords for discovery. New title adds 'Equipment Tracking' (searchable) + 'Campus Management' (matches education persona). Description adds 'open source, free to start' which drove +150-180% CTR in batch 1.

experiments.before: Free multi-location asset tracking software. Track equipment across offices, warehouses, campuses, and job sites with QR scanning and real-time location updates. Open source.
experiments.after: Track equipment across offices, warehouses, campuses, and job sites from one dashboard. QR scanning, location transfers, and custody tracking. Free to start, open source.
experiments.hypothesis: New page (2026-03-12), establishing baseline. Changed 'Free multi-location...' to 'Track equipment... from one dashboard' — leads with the benefit (unified visibility) rather than claiming the whole product is free. 'Free to start' is accurate (free tier includes locations). Multi-location tracking IS free on the free tier.

experiments.before: Free maintenance tracking for equipment teams. Schedule preventive maintenance, log service history, and get automatic reminders — without CMMS complexity. Open source.
experiments.after: Lightweight maintenance tracking — not a full CMMS. Schedule preventive maintenance, log service history, and get automatic reminders. Open source, free to start.
experiments.hypothesis: TRUTHFULNESS FIX: Removed 'for equipment teams' (free tier is single-user). Replaced with 'Lightweight maintenance tracking — not a full CMMS' which is the page's actual positioning and differentiates from UpKeep/Limble/Fiix on SERP. 'Free to start' is accurate.

experiments.before: Title: 'Home Inventory Management Software | Tag & Track Belongings | Shelf' / Desc: 'Tag and track your household items with QR codes. Perfect for insurance documentation, moving, and organization.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Free Home Inventory App — QR Tag & Track Everything You Own | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free home inventory software. Tag belongings with QR codes, track by room and location, attach photos and receipts. Perfect for insurance claims, moves, and estate planning.'
experiments.hypothesis: Home inventory is a perfect free-tier use case (single user managing personal belongings). 'Free' is 100% accurate here. Added 'App' to match mobile-first search intent. Expanded description with specific use cases (insurance claims, estate planning) to match long-tail queries.

experiments.before: Title: 'Mobile Asset Auditing Software | Scan & Audit On-The-Go | Shelf' / Desc: 'Audit inventory from your phone or tablet. Scan QR labels, verify locations, and generate compliance reports in the field.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Mobile Asset Audit Software — Scan QR Labels from Any Phone | Shelf' / Desc: 'Audit assets from any phone or tablet. Scan QR labels, verify locations, update records, and generate compliance reports on the go. Free to start, open source.'
experiments.hypothesis: Current title uses pipe separators (old pattern). New title uses em dash (consistent with site). 'from Any Phone' emphasizes no-app-needed advantage. Added 'Free to start, open source' to description (batch 1 winning formula).

experiments.before: Replace Excel and Google Sheets for equipment tracking. Shelf gives you QR scanning, automatic custody logs, booking calendars, and real-time availability — free to start.
experiments.after: Replace Excel and Google Sheets for equipment tracking. QR scanning, automatic custody logs, and real-time availability — no more version conflicts. Free to start, open source.
experiments.hypothesis: Removed 'booking calendars' from feature list (Team-only feature, misleading in 'free to start' context). Added 'no more version conflicts' — the #1 pain point of spreadsheet users. Added 'open source' (batch 1 winning signal).

experiments.before: Title: 'Equipment Booking & Reservation Software | Shelf' / Desc: 'Schedule equipment without conflicts using Shelf's simple booking workflows for cameras, tools, laptops, and shared resources.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Equipment Booking Software — Conflict-Free Reservations | Shelf' / Desc: 'Reserve equipment without double-bookings. Visual booking calendar, availability checking, and pull-list PDFs for cameras, tools, laptops, and shared gear.'
experiments.hypothesis: 1,111 impr, 1.9% CTR, pos 10.4. Title uses old pipe format; updated to em dash (consistent). 'Conflict-Free Reservations' is a stronger value prop than '& Reservation Software'. Description adds specific features (visual calendar, pull-list PDFs) instead of generic 'simple booking workflows'. No 'Free' claim — bookings is a paid feature.
